blurb: The Tony Blair Institute for Global Change is a UK-founded policy advisory organisation that publishes prolifically on AI in government, public services, and geopolitics, generally arguing for aggressive state adoption of AI.

publications.title: Europe in the Age of AI: How Technology Leadership Can Boost Competitiveness and Security
publications.summary: Argues Europe can strengthen its economy and strategic autonomy by scaling digital infrastructure, embracing AI, and reforming tech regulation rather than defaulting to a precautionary regulatory posture.

publications.title: AI-Native Biotech Is an Opportunity for UK Leadership and Growth
publications.summary: Contends a new era of AI-native biotech is emerging and the UK's sovereign assets make it well placed to host these wealth-generating companies if it acts to attract them.

publications.title: What the UK Thinks About AI: Building Public Trust to Accelerate Adoption
publications.summary: Presents new polling arguing that AI can transform public services but that public trust and understanding must first be improved to unlock faster adoption.

publications.title: Digital Embassies and AI Sovereignty: Building Resilient States Beyond Borders
publications.byline: Jared Wright, Ott Velsberg, Alexander Iosad
publications.summary: Proposes 'digital embassies' as a strategic mechanism for states to maintain resilience, security, sovereignty and operational continuity in the age of AI.

publications.title: Rebooting Copyright: How the UK Can Be a Global Leader in the Arts and AI
publications.byline: Jakob Mökander, Amanda Brock, Mick Grierson
publications.summary: Argues the UK can support artists, encourage responsible AI use and unlock economic growth by modernising its copyright law for the AI era.

publications.title: Governing in the Age of AI: Reimagining Local Government
publications.summary: Argues local authorities can harness AI to support staff, improve citizens' access to services and streamline decision-making to improve efficiency and outcomes.

notes: Harvested from institute.global sitemap.xml (200 OK, browser UA). Sitemap lastmod values are crawl timestamps (mostly 2026-06-23); used each page's JSON-LD datePublished as the true publication date instead. TBI is prolific on AI policy; selected six recent, high-signal, clearly AI-focused insights with confirmed datePublished + author. No controversies found where TBI is a named legal/regulatory party. No first-party press-release feed parsed.
